name: SQL Injection
description: Attacker exploits improperly sanitized input to inject SQL commands into backend database queries

name: XSS via HTTP Headers
description: Attacker injects malicious content via HTTP response headers that is rendered by the client browser

name: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS)
description: Attacker injects malicious scripts into web pages viewed by other users

name: Phishing
description: Attacker creates fake communications that appear to come from a trusted source to trick victims

name: Brute Force
description: Attacker attempts to gain access by trying many passwords or passphrases systematically

name: Privilege Abuse
description: Attacker exploits features of the target that they are already authorized to use to perform unintended actions

name: Path Traversal
description: Attacker manipulates file path references to access files outside the intended directory

name: Input Data Manipulation
description: Attacker modifies data sent to the application to achieve unauthorized actions

name: Session Credential Falsification through Forging
description: Attacker creates fraudulent session credentials to impersonate a legitimate user

name: Privilege Escalation
description: Attacker exploits a weakness to gain elevated access to resources normally protected

name: Code Injection
description: Attacker introduces code into a program to alter its execution

name: HTTP Response Smuggling
description: Attacker manipulates HTTP responses to inject malicious content or cause cache poisoning

name: Network Topology Mapping
description: Attacker maps the target network to identify hosts, services, and potential entry points

name: Creating a Rogue Certification Authority Certificate
description: Attacker installs a rogue CA certificate to perform man-in-the-middle attacks

name: Use of Known Domain Credentials
description: Attacker uses previously obtained valid credentials to authenticate to a domain

name: Credential Stuffing
description: Attacker systematically tries stolen username/password pairs against target authentication systems

name: Server Side Request Forgery (SSRF)
description: Attacker abuses server functionality to make requests to internal resources

name: NoSQL Injection
description: Attacker injects malicious queries into NoSQL database operations

name: Exploiting Incorrectly Configured Access Control Security Levels
description: Attacker exploits access control misconfigurations to gain unauthorized access

name: Excavation
description: Attacker probes the target system to gather as much information as possible about its configuration
